title: Fallout 3
notes: Runs well at 1920x1080, looks good, sounds great.  xlive.dll needed in data folder in order to run.  Minor graphics "white spots" (textures?  shaders?) corrected by turning off HDR setting in graphics options.

Error on installation. Got an error message ''You must use "Turn Windows features on or off" in the Control Panel to install or configure Microsoft .NET Framework 3.0'. Installing dotnet30 using wine didn't work, complained of installing a 32-bit library into a 64-bit drive. Launcher works, but crashes upon clicking play. Putting xlive.dll in game folder didn't work, neither did trying to disable GFWL by installing FOSE and running it from the FOSE launcher.
title: Fallout 3

gpu: X.Org AMD Radeon (TM) RX 460 Graphics (POLARIS11 / DRM 3.23.0 / 4.15.0-34-generic, LLVM 6.0.0)
notes: Launcher works, but game not responding
title: Fallout 3

notes: The culprit is Games for Windows Live.  I followed the example ( tweaked for my system ) at https://steamcommunity.com/app/22370/discussions/0/1736594593607083669/
title: Fallout 3

notes: As other people have stated it works out of the box if you place xlive.dll into the game's main folder, usually $HOME/.local/share/Steam/steamapps/common/Fallout\ 3/
title: Fallout 3

notes: Install fallout 3 like you would normally. NOTE: DO NOT RUN THE GAME YET! Your system can lock up and you might experience a kernel panic. I had to hard reset when this happened since I got locked out. Open a terminal and (1) $ sudo apt update && sudo apt install msitools (2) $ cd SteamLibrary/steamapps/common/Fallout 3/GFWLive (3) $ msiextract xliveredist.msi (4) $ cp SysFldr/xlive.dll SysFldr/xlive.dll.cat SysFldr/xlivefnt.dll ../ (5) Close the terminal, go to steam library, and right click fallout 3, click properties, in general click set launch options, and copy and paste, PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command%, to disable esync. Click ok, close, and done. Now you can run fallout 3 and it should be playable. I experienced occasional crashes. I only played up until I left the vault and wandered the wasteland for a few minutes. Enjoy :)
title: Fallout 3

notes: I copied and pasted xlive.dll into the main folder as seen in previous reports. Not sure if it is the drivers or the underpowered GPU that makes it crash. Surprisingly further along in compatibility than on Windows 10 1803.
